_comment: Canonical Claude Code hooks block for agent VMs. Single source of truth: base/09 composes ~/.claude/settings.json from it at provision time, and the-assistant docs/agents/scripts/update-agent.sh re-merges it onto existing boxes (base/09 is not re-run there). Commands are literal — $HOME/$PORTAL_URL/$AGENT_TOKEN/$AGENT_NAME expand when Claude runs the hook. The last-tool-use writer, the tool-event forwarder, the stop hook, and the needs-input request forwarder (all installed by base/14) each gate themselves to the JOB session by comparing their stdin session_id against job-context.json so lingering/operator sessions cannot pollute job liveness or reporting (JOB-SIGNAL-ATTRIBUTION plan). The .* tool-event forwarder posts a jq-REDUCED payload (byte-derived token estimates, never raw tool_input/tool_response) to /api/agents/events for per-MCP-server attribution (SCRUM-512); it skips TaskCreate/TaskUpdate, which the dedicated entry posts in full. sb-post-request.sh (SCRUM-1373) makes a BLOCKED agent visible in the portal: PreToolUse AskUserQuestion|ExitPlanMode opens a needs-input request, the Notification hook opens permission/idle requests, and PostToolUse AskUserQuestion|ExitPlanMode + the Stop hook resolve them — POSTed reduced + backgrounded + silent to /api/agents/requests.
